stakes.your_upside: Salary increase to $195-210K range, staying with preferred team and company trajectory
stakes.your_downside: Remaining at below-market $175K, potential resentment, or being forced to take external offer and leave preferred environment
stakes.their_upside: Retaining high-performing engineer who delivered $2M in annual savings at reasonable cost increase
stakes.their_downside: Losing proven performer to competitor, disrupting team dynamics, and facing expensive replacement/knowledge transfer costs

timeline.decision_deadline: Unknown; likely within 2-4 weeks based on typical review cycles and competing offer timeline

power_dynamics.who_needs_whom_more: Roughly balanced - you have strong external alternative but prefer to stay; they need to retain proven talent in competitive market
power_dynamics.information_asymmetry: You have advantage knowing exact competing offer details and peer salary data; they likely know company budget constraints and promotion pipeline
power_dynamics.relationship_value: High for both parties - you want to stay with this team, they want to retain someone who knows systems and delivers results

your_anxiety_points: Being perceived as disloyal for having external offer
Manager not having budget authority for meaningful increase
Damaging relationship with preferred team if negotiation goes poorly
their_likely_anxiety_points: Losing high performer who delivered major cost savings
Setting precedent for other engineers with external offers
